data.title: Tesla technical analysis - RSI indicator in overbought territory
data.description:
Tesla's (NASDAQ:TSLA) Relative Strength Indicator is at 71.4, indicating an overbought territory. 

RSI is a momentum indicator that measures the magnitude of recent price changes to read whether the stock is overbought or oversold. An RSI score above 70 depicts overbought, and its price may fall, whereas an RSI score below 30 means it could be oversold and might correct upwards.

Looking back, the last time the RSI indicator scaled above 70 was in December 2024 when it touched an all-time high of $479 and receded thereafter. In March 2025, the RSI indicator fell below 30, and upward price revision was seen after that.

 

 

 

 

Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) stock price crossed its upper Bollinger Band on May 12, when the stock price scaled above $315, once again giving an overbought signal.

The Bollinger Bands indicator works with three bands, consisting of the middle band that tracks average price, the upper band that is average price plus two standard deviations, and the lower band that is average price minus two standard deviations.

Importantly, the stock price breaking the upper band suggests an overbought scenario, while falling below the lower band is an oversold indicator.

Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) shares rose for six straight days but snapped the winning streak on Thursday. The rally has shrunk the stock’s YTD loss to about 15%. 

Turning to the Wall Street community, almost 16 analysts gave TSLA a Strong Buy while 7 stood with Buy whereas, 14 of them gave the stock a Hold recommendation, and 8 of them gave a Strong Sell rating.

data.title: Eli Lilly, compounders partly to blame for Novo CEO’s ouster
data.description:
Novo Nordisk (NVO) (OTCPK:NONOF) appears to have forced its CEO, Lars Fruergaard Jørgensen, to resign as the Danish pharma giant grapples with the rising dominance of its U.S. rival, Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY), and makers of compounded drugs in the booming market for weight loss therapies.

“Considering the recent market challenges, the share price decline, the Novo Nordisk Board and Lars Fruergaard Jørgensen have jointly concluded that initiating a CEO succession is in the best interest of the company and its shareholders,” the company said in a statement.

Input from the Novo Nordisk Foundation, which controls the company through its investment arm, Novo Holdings A/S, has also led to the decision.

However, multiple media outlets reported that Jørgensen was ousted. The Financial Times reported that the request to step down had surprised Jørgensen, who had, however, agreed with the board’s decision.

Under Jørgensen’s eight-year tenure as CEO, Novo’s (NVO) sales, profits, and share price have almost tripled despite the recent underperformance marked by LLY’s recent market share gains amid plateauing weekly U.S. prescriptions of the company's weight loss drug, Wegovy.

According to IQVIA data, the weekly U.S. prescriptions of Lilly’s (NYSE:LLY) weight loss therapy Zepbound have approached 339K for the week ending April 18, surpassing those of Wegovy by more than 127K. The Indiana-based drugmaker has also captured 63% of the U.S. weight loss drug market, Reuters reported, citing IQVIA data.

“It’s easy with hindsight to look back and see things that didn’t turn out exactly as intended,” Jørgensen told FT, adding that he had no regrets about the decisions made as the CEO.

Novo (NVO), once Europe’s largest company in terms of market cap, thanks to its highly popular weight loss drug semaglutide, marketed as Ozempic and Wegovy, has lost more than half of its value since mid-2024.

Meanwhile, Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) has lost ~20% as both companies struggled to keep up with the surging demand for their weight loss drugs, partly met by compounders such as Hims & Hers Health (NYSE:HIMS) during supply constraints.

While the shortages are officially over in the U.S., Novo Nordisk (NVO) lowered its 2025 growth outlook for sales and operating profit this month, citing a longer-than-expected impact from compounded versions.

With its leadership now in transition amid LLY's market share gains, investors will be keenly watching how the company can return to its former glory.

data.title: Analysts say the Charter-Cox merger is a 'no-brainer' and 'major positive'
data.description:
Oppenheimer upgraded shares of Charter Communications (NASDAQ:CHTR) to "outperform" after the company on Friday announced a merger deal with Cox Communications, which has an enterprise value of $34.5B.

The research firm said it had taken an incrementally more positive stance on CHTR over the past few quarters for its improved services and bundles, particularly attractive OTT video and wireless packages, and now views the Cox transaction as "a major positive" and a catalyst.

"CHTR continues to upgrade its wireline network to enable multi-gigabit broadband speeds. As such, it is in a period of elevated CapEx that will step down materially in the next two years. Channel checks suggest Cox's network is also in good shape. We expect the combined networks will bring more scale to market the brand and pursue bundling across a wider footprint," Oppenheimer analysts said Friday.

Pivotal Research, on the other hand, calls the Charter-Cox deal a "no-brainer." They said the transaction implies "a very attractive valuation," which will materially accelerate growth, and they do not see any regulatory issues with the merger. 

The research firm said Charter's operating strategies "are absolutely transferable to the Cox footprint" and will be able to accelerate Cox's core results. They also expect Charter shareholders to get a boost from the inexpensive price paid for Cox, along with significant synergies. 

Pivotal upped Charter's price target to $600 from $540, implying an upside of 43%, while Oppenheimer sees an upside of 19% with a $500 PT.

data.title: Lynas achieves first heavy rare earths production outside China
data.description:
Lynas Rare Earths (OTCPK:LYSCF) (OTCPK:LYSDY) has achieved first production of dysprosium oxide at its Malaysian plant, making it the only commercial producer of separated heavy rare earths outside China, Argus reported Friday.

Dysprosium is a material considered essential for high-performance magnets used in electric vehicles and wind turbines.

The company's first production of dysprosium comes shortly after the Chinese government restricted exports of some rare earth minerals, including dysprosium and terbium.

Lynas (OTCPK:LYSCF) (OTCPK:LYSDY) built dysprosium and terbium processing circuits capable of separating as much as 1,500 metric tons/year of heavy rare earths at the Malaysian plant during the March quarter, with first production of separated terbium oxide at the site expected in June.

The circuits will allow the company to eventually expand its heavy rare earth production line to include separated dysprosium, terbium, and holmium concentrate, as well as unseparated samarium/europium/gadolinium and unseparated mixed heavy rare earths, Argus explained.

data.title: Quantum computing stocks rise gain amid industry earnings
data.description:
• Shares of quantum computing stocks rose on Friday after stronger-than-expected results from industry player, Quantum Computing (NASDAQ:QUBT).
  • Quantum Computing shares rose more than 36% after the company said it earned $0.11 per share during the first-quarter on $39,000 in revenue. 
  • Other industry names, such as IonQ (NYSE:IONQ), D-Wave Quantum (QBTS), Arqit Quantum (NASDAQ:ARQQ) and Rigetti Computing (RGTI) also rose on Friday. 
  • IonQ also topped first-quarter estimates earlier in the month. It also expects full-year revenue to be between $75M and $95M, compared to the $85.42M estimate.

data.title: OpenAI launches preview of software engineering AI agent Codex
data.description:
OpenAI continues to develop new artificial intelligence tools as it launched a research preview today of Codex, a cloud-based, software engineering AI agent.

Accessed through a sidebar in ChatGPT, Codex can "perform tasks for you such as writing features, answering questions about your codebase, fixing bugs, and proposing pull requests for review; each task runs in its own cloud sandbox environment, preloaded with your repository," the company said.

It is powered by codex-1, a version of OpenAI o3 optimized for software engineering.

"It was trained using reinforcement learning on real-world coding tasks in a variety of environments to generate code that closely mirrors human style and PR preferences, adheres precisely to instructions, and can iteratively run tests until it receives a passing result," said the startup .

The research preview version is available today for ChatGPT Pro, Team and Enterprise users. It will roll out to Plus and Edu users soon.

The company, which is backed heavily by Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) and SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY), is currently trying to restructure itself from a nonprofit into a public benefit corporation, which could help it eventually launch an initial public offering.

data.title: Iovance downgraded to Neutral at UBS related to Amtagvi concerns
data.description:
Iovance Biotherapeutics (NASDAQ:IOVA) has been downgraded to Neutral from Buy at UBS, reflecting growing uncertainty around the commercial rollout of its cell therapy, Amtagvi. 

The analysts at UBS say that despite the initial excitement following approval, recent financial and operational updates suggest a slower-than-anticipated ramp, raising concerns about the treatment’s near-term uptake and long-term market potential.

"With a lack of near-term visibility of Amtagvi ramp, we move to the sidelines and downgrade to Neutral. We reduce PT to $2 based on 0.6x  EV/'30E multiple." UBS analysts said.

The company reported first-quarter revenue this month of $49.3M, below the consensus of $81.53M, after the scheduled maintenance at the Iovance Cell Therapy Center sharply cut the production capacity. 

The stock of the company is down 5% so far today.

data.title: Entrepreneur Universe Bright Group reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Entrepreneur Universe Bright Group press release (OTCQB:EUBG): Q1 revenue of $0.96M (-23.8% Y/Y).
  • Net Income: $183,485 in Q1 2025, down 50.9% from $373,496 in Q1 2024.

data.title: Fortnite blocked on all iOS devices; Apple asks Epic to resubmit game update sans U.S.
data.description:
Fortnite, the popular multiplayer online shooting game, has been made unavailable by Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) following Epic Games' submission for the latest update that was initially put on hold for 24 hours by the tech giant. 

"Apple has blocked our Fortnite submission so we cannot release to the US App Store or to the Epic Games Store for iOS in the European Union. Now, sadly, Fortnite on iOS will be offline worldwide until Apple unblocks it," according to an official statement on X on Friday.

Apple said it has asked Epic Games to exclude the update for the U.S. and make a resubmission while also clarifying that It did not take action to remove Fortnite in the EU.

Back in 2020, Fortnite was taken down by Apple and Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) for a guideline violation after Epic introduced a payment feature to bypass the 30% commission imposed by the companies.

But after a four-year hiatus, Fortnite was made available on iOS in the EU and on Android devices globally after Epic launched its games store. Epic said it would charge a store fee of 12% for payments processed and 0% on third-party payments. 

Earlier this year, Apple blocked Epic's developer account after losing faith in the game company's ability to fulfill contractual obligations and called them "verifiably untrustworthy" in a letter.

"In terminating Epic’s developer account, Apple is taking out one of the largest potential competitors to the Apple App Store," Epic had said then.

data.title: Macy's declares $0.1824 dividend
data.description:
